CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-01-2022 Continued To Apr-20-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoena For Production Of Business Records
Off Calendar Dec-29-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

THIRD PARTY ADULT PROTECTIVE SERVICES' Motion To Quash Subpoena For Production Of Business Records is Transferred to be heard on the discovery calendar (room 301) on the same date at 9:00 a.m.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Third Party Adult Protective Services' Motion To Quash Subpoena For Production Of Business Records
Off Calendar Dec-29-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. BRADLEY L. BOSTICK, Esq. of Mediation and Law Office of Bradley Bostick (Tel: 510-645-1575) for plaintiffs;
Atty. DAVID K. RIES, Esq. of San Francisco City Attorney's Office (Tel: 415-554-3939) for third party witness Adult Protective Services.

Hearing Re: Third Party Adult Protective Services' Motion To Quash Subpoena For Production Of Business Records.

Plaintiffs and third party witness stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for plaintiffs and third party witness authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

THIRD PARTY ADULT PROTECTION SERVICES ("APS") MOTION TO QUASH THE PLAINTIFF'S SUBPOENS SEEKING PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS IS DENIED. THE APS IS ORDERED TO PRODUCE ALL POTENTIALLY RELEVANT TO THE PENDING LITIGATION SUBJECT TO A PROTECTIVE ORDER AGREED TO BY THE PARTIES.

Counsel for plaintiffs to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Judge Pro Tem at rennepar85@gmail.com along with the parties' stipulated protective order.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
